l1_blocks.title: Peak Staffing Model
l1_blocks.description: Creates a data-driven staffing model that matches team deployment to customer demand patterns throughout the day, ensuring the right number of people with the right skills are on the floor at the right times — particularly during peak trading periods.

l1_blocks.title: Morning Rush Workflow
l1_blocks.description: Designs and optimises the workflow for the morning rush period — typically 7:30 to 9:30am — defining roles, positions, task sequences, and communication protocols that enable the team to serve the maximum number of customers at speed without sacrificing drink quality or customer experience.

l1_blocks.title: Pre-Rush Mise-en-Place
l1_blocks.description: Establishes a comprehensive pre-rush preparation protocol — the cafe equivalent of culinary mise en place — ensuring that every supply, tool, and station element is in position and ready before peak service begins, so the team can focus entirely on speed and quality during the rush.

l1_blocks.title: Peak Queue Management
l1_blocks.description: Provides systems and strategies for managing customer queues during peak periods — morning rush, lunchtime, and weekend surges — to minimise wait times, prevent walkaways, and maintain service quality under pressure. This block turns the queue from a pain point into a managed, positive experience.

l1_blocks.title: Order-Taking Speed Protocol
l1_blocks.description: Streamlines the order-taking process to minimise the time between a customer reaching the till and their order being entered into the system, without sacrificing warmth, accuracy, or the opportunity to upsell. This block focuses on the till interaction as the primary bottleneck in cafe throughput.

l1_blocks.title: Queue Communication Experience
l1_blocks.description: Transforms the waiting experience for customers in the queue by proactively communicating wait times, maintaining visual progress, and creating small positive moments that reduce perceived wait time. This block ensures that even when customers wait, they feel informed, valued, and engaged.

l1_blocks.title: Seating Turnover Strategy
l1_blocks.description: Develops strategies to optimise table turnover during peak periods without making customers feel rushed, balancing the need to maximise covers with the cafe's hospitality values. This block addresses the delicate art of moving people through without pushing them out.

l1_blocks.title: Peak Capacity Management
l1_blocks.description: Creates a systematic approach to managing the cafe at maximum capacity — coordinating queue management, seating allocation, service speed, kitchen throughput, and team deployment to ensure that peak periods generate maximum revenue without collapsing into chaos.

l1_blocks.title: Daily Prep List Protocol
l1_blocks.description: Establishes a structured daily preparation list system that ensures every shift begins with a complete, prioritised checklist of prep tasks — from food preparation and ingredient restocking to station setup and mise en place. This block eliminates the guesswork and memory dependence that causes prep failures, rushed mornings, and service breakdowns during peak hours.

l1_blocks.title: Machine Maintenance Log
l1_blocks.description: Establishes a daily equipment maintenance logging system that tracks the condition, cleaning, and servicing of all critical cafe machinery — espresso machines, grinders, dishwashers, refrigeration units, ovens, and blenders. This block prevents costly breakdowns, extends equipment lifespan, and ensures every drink and dish is produced by properly maintained machinery.

l1_blocks.title: Waste Log & Reconciliation
l1_blocks.description: Establishes a disciplined daily waste logging system that captures every item discarded — from expired pastries and spoilt milk to over-extracted shots and dropped cups — then reconciles waste costs against revenue to reveal hidden profit leaks. This block transforms waste from an invisible cost into a measurable, manageable, and continuously improvable metric.

l1_blocks.title: Daily Team Briefing Protocol
l1_blocks.description: Establishes a structured pre-shift team briefing — a daily five-to-ten-minute huddle that aligns the team on priorities, shares critical information, surfaces concerns, and sets the tone for the shift ahead.

l1_blocks.title: Weekly Team Meeting Structure
l1_blocks.description: Establishes a structured weekly team meeting that goes beyond daily briefings to address operational review, problem-solving, training, and team development in a dedicated 30-to-45-minute session that keeps the team engaged and the business improving.

l1_blocks.title: Seating Layout Optimisation
l1_blocks.description: Optimises the cafe's seating layout to maximise both revenue capacity and customer comfort, ensuring the space works hard for the business while creating an environment where customers want to linger, return, and spend. This block treats seating as a strategic asset that directly affects revenue per square foot.

l1_blocks.title: Lighting, Music & Ambiance
l1_blocks.description: Creates and manages the sensory atmosphere of the cafe — lighting, music, temperature, and scent — so that the environment emotionally supports the brand positioning, enhances the customer experience, and influences behaviour in ways that benefit the business. This block recognises that atmosphere is not a background detail but a primary product that customers are paying for.

l2_blocks.title: Peak Staffing Model
l2_blocks.description: Creates a data-driven staffing model that matches team deployment to customer demand patterns throughout the day, ensuring the right number of people with the right skills are on the floor at the right times — particularly during peak trading periods.

l2_blocks.title: Morning Rush Workflow
l2_blocks.description: Designs and optimises the workflow for the morning rush period — typically 7:30 to 9:30am — defining roles, positions, task sequences, and communication protocols that enable the team to serve the maximum number of customers at speed without sacrificing drink quality or customer experience.

l2_blocks.title: Pre-Rush Mise-en-Place
l2_blocks.description: Establishes a comprehensive pre-rush preparation protocol — the cafe equivalent of culinary mise en place — ensuring that every supply, tool, and station element is in position and ready before peak service begins, so the team can focus entirely on speed and quality during the rush.

l2_blocks.title: Peak Performance Analysis
l2_blocks.description: Establishes a structured approach to measuring, analysing, and improving peak period performance using data from transactions, service times, waste, customer feedback, and team observations to drive continuous workflow optimisation.

l2_blocks.title: Drive-Through Workflow
l2_blocks.description: Designs and optimises the complete drive-through workflow from order point to window handoff, ensuring speed of service, order accuracy, and a positive customer experience within the unique constraints of vehicle-based service. This block addresses lane management, communication systems, menu board design, staffing positions, and performance metrics specific to drive-through cafe operations.

l2_blocks.title: Kiosk Operations
l2_blocks.description: Establishes the operational framework for running a coffee kiosk or compact format venue, addressing the unique challenges of limited space, reduced equipment, minimal staff, and high-footfall locations. This block covers workflow design within tight footprints, condensed menu strategies, efficient stock management, and the operational discipline required when there is no back-of-house to fall back on.

l2_blocks.title: Coffee Van Operations
l2_blocks.description: Addresses the unique operational logistics of running a mobile coffee van, covering vehicle maintenance, location strategy, event booking, licensing, self-contained water and power systems, setup and teardown routines, and the mobile-specific challenges that differentiate van operations from any fixed-site format.

l2_blocks.title: Peak Queue Management
l2_blocks.description: Provides systems and strategies for managing customer queues during peak periods — morning rush, lunchtime, and weekend surges — to minimise wait times, prevent walkaways, and maintain service quality under pressure. This block turns the queue from a pain point into a managed, positive experience.

l2_blocks.title: Order-Taking Speed Protocol
l2_blocks.description: Streamlines the order-taking process to minimise the time between a customer reaching the till and their order being entered into the system, without sacrificing warmth, accuracy, or the opportunity to upsell. This block focuses on the till interaction as the primary bottleneck in cafe throughput.

l2_blocks.title: Queue Communication Experience
l2_blocks.description: Transforms the waiting experience for customers in the queue by proactively communicating wait times, maintaining visual progress, and creating small positive moments that reduce perceived wait time. This block ensures that even when customers wait, they feel informed, valued, and engaged.

l2_blocks.title: Throughput Optimisation System
l2_blocks.description: Creates a systematic approach to measuring, analysing, and improving the cafe's customer throughput — the number of customers served per hour — through workflow analysis, bottleneck identification, and operational refinement. This block treats throughput as a measurable, improvable metric rather than a fixed characteristic.

l2_blocks.title: Seating Turnover Strategy
l2_blocks.description: Develops strategies to optimise table turnover during peak periods without making customers feel rushed, balancing the need to maximise covers with the cafe's hospitality values. This block addresses the delicate art of moving people through without pushing them out.

l2_blocks.title: Peak Capacity Management
l2_blocks.description: Creates a systematic approach to managing the cafe at maximum capacity — coordinating queue management, seating allocation, service speed, kitchen throughput, and team deployment to ensure that peak periods generate maximum revenue without collapsing into chaos.

l2_blocks.title: Seating Experience Optimisation
l2_blocks.description: Balances the tension between maximising seating turnover for revenue and providing a comfortable, welcoming experience that encourages return visits. This capstone block integrates turnover strategy (B098) and capacity management (B099) into a holistic seating philosophy that serves both the business and the customer.

l2_blocks.title: Daily Prep List Protocol
l2_blocks.description: Establishes a structured daily preparation list system that ensures every shift begins with a complete, prioritised checklist of prep tasks — from food preparation and ingredient restocking to station setup and mise en place. This block eliminates the guesswork and memory dependence that causes prep failures, rushed mornings, and service breakdowns during peak hours.

l2_blocks.title: Machine Maintenance Log
l2_blocks.description: Establishes a daily equipment maintenance logging system that tracks the condition, cleaning, and servicing of all critical cafe machinery — espresso machines, grinders, dishwashers, refrigeration units, ovens, and blenders. This block prevents costly breakdowns, extends equipment lifespan, and ensures every drink and dish is produced by properly maintained machinery.

l2_blocks.title: Waste Log & Reconciliation
l2_blocks.description: Establishes a disciplined daily waste logging system that captures every item discarded — from expired pastries and spoilt milk to over-extracted shots and dropped cups — then reconciles waste costs against revenue to reveal hidden profit leaks. This block transforms waste from an invisible cost into a measurable, manageable, and continuously improvable metric.

l2_blocks.title: Operational Rhythm Dashboard
l2_blocks.description: Creates a single-view operational dashboard that consolidates daily KPIs — revenue, labour cost, waste, speed of service, customer count, and compliance checks — into a real-time pulse of cafe performance. This block ensures the manager never has to guess how the day is going and can intervene before small problems become expensive ones.

l2_blocks.title: Daily Team Briefing Protocol
l2_blocks.description: Establishes a structured pre-shift team briefing — a daily five-to-ten-minute huddle that aligns the team on priorities, shares critical information, surfaces concerns, and sets the tone for the shift ahead.

l2_blocks.title: Weekly Team Meeting Structure
l2_blocks.description: Establishes a structured weekly team meeting that goes beyond daily briefings to address operational review, problem-solving, training, and team development in a dedicated 30-to-45-minute session that keeps the team engaged and the business improving.

l2_blocks.title: Communication Culture & Feedback
l2_blocks.description: Builds an open, psychologically safe communication culture where feedback flows freely in all directions — upward, downward, and peer-to-peer — ensuring issues are raised early, good work is recognised, and the team operates with transparency and mutual respect.

l2_blocks.title: Seating Layout Optimisation
l2_blocks.description: Optimises the cafe's seating layout to maximise both revenue capacity and customer comfort, ensuring the space works hard for the business while creating an environment where customers want to linger, return, and spend. This block treats seating as a strategic asset that directly affects revenue per square foot.

l2_blocks.title: Lighting, Music & Ambiance
l2_blocks.description: Creates and manages the sensory atmosphere of the cafe — lighting, music, temperature, and scent — so that the environment emotionally supports the brand positioning, enhances the customer experience, and influences behaviour in ways that benefit the business. This block recognises that atmosphere is not a background detail but a primary product that customers are paying for.

l2_blocks.title: Atmosphere Design Programme
l2_blocks.description: Establishes a comprehensive approach to designing and refining the cafe's atmosphere, encompassing lighting, acoustics, furniture, colour palette, and sensory experience. Ensures the physical environment actively supports the brand identity and encourages longer dwell times and repeat visits.
